title: Polar Research Base: Footprint Selection
strategies: single-cell baseline: stake the whole base on the highest-value grid cell; reproduces the evaluator's weak reference and scores ~0.1

single-seed region growing: grow from the best cell, annexing the boundary cell of largest positive net gain (v - perim_cost*(4-2k)) until no improving move remains, capturing one hotspot cluster but stalling at its local optimum
multi-seed region growing: run greedy growth from EVERY hotspot seed and keep the best footprint, since the strongest region often surrounds a hotspot other than the global maximum cell

seeded add-only simulated annealing: from the best grown region, accept small negative-gain annexations with probability exp(gain/T) to pay short bridges of mild-cost ice toward nearby hotspot clusters, keeping a best-net snapshot
